static_findings.description:
CircuitBreakerVault.emergencyWithdraw(address,uint256) (../../../../../tmp/sentinel_static_gxjcc1be.sol#89-94) sends eth to arbitrary user
	Dangerous calls:
	- (ok,None) = user.call{value: amount}() (../../../../../tmp/sentinel_static_gxjcc1be.sol#92)
CircuitBreakerVault.pauseThreshold (../../../../../tmp/sentinel_static_gxjcc1be.sol#15) should be constant

Version constraint ^0.8.0 contains known severe issues (https://solidity.readthedocs.io/en/latest/bugs.html)
	- FullInlinerNonExpressionSplitArgumentEvaluationOrder
	- MissingSideEffectsOnSelectorAccess
	- AbiReencodingHeadOverflowWithStaticArrayCleanup
	- DirtyBytesArrayToStorage
	- DataLocationChangeInInternalOverride
	- NestedCalldataArrayAbiReencodingSizeValidation
	- SignedImmutables
	- ABIDecodeTwoDimensionalArrayMemory
	- KeccakCaching.
It is used by:
	- ^0.8.0 (../../../../../tmp/sentinel_static_gxjcc1be.sol#7)

Low level call in CircuitBreakerVault.withdraw(uint256) (../../../../../tmp/sentinel_static_gxjcc1be.sol#46-52):
	- (ok,None) = msg.sender.call{value: amount}() (../../../../../tmp/sentinel_static_gxjcc1be.sol#50)

Low level call in CircuitBreakerVault.emergencyWithdraw(address,uint256) (../../../../../tmp/sentinel_static_gxjcc1be.sol#89-94):
	- (ok,None) = user.call{value: amount}() (../../../../../tmp/sentinel_static_gxjcc1be.sol#92)
Consider introducing checks for `msg.sender` to ensure the recipient of the money is as intended.

The use of keccak256 hash functions on predictable values like block.timestamp, block.number, or similar data, including modulo operations on these values, should be avoided for generating randomness, as they are easily predictable and manipulable. The `PREVRANDAO` opcode also should not be used as a source of randomness. Instead, utilize Chainlink VRF for cryptographically secure and provably random values to ensure protocol integrity.
Define and use `constant` variables instead of using literals. If the same constant literal value is used multiple times, create a constant state variable and reference it throughout the contract.

Solc compiler version 0.8.20 switches the default target EVM version to Shanghai, which means that the generated bytecode will include PUSH0 opcodes. Be sure to select the appropriate EVM version in case you intend to deploy on a chain other than mainnet like L2 chains that may not support PUSH0, otherwise deployment of your contracts will fail.
There are state variable changes in this function but no event is emitted. Consider emitting an event to enable offchain indexers to track the changes.
State variables that are not updated following deployment should be declared constant to save gas. Add the `constant` attribute to state variables that never change.
Consider using a specific version of Solidity in your contracts instead of a wide version. For example, instead of `pragma solidity ^0.8.0;`, use `pragma solidity 0.8.0;`
